patches.find: Aubert et al., 2013, Nature), which reproduce hemispheric asymmetry via core-mantle boundary heat flux variations and reversed-flux patches. (Note: these simulations are data-constrained numerical models, not purely first-principles derivations — but they are derived from Maxwell's equations and Navier-Stokes, with boundary conditions from seismology.)
patches.replace: Olson & Deguen, 2012, Nature Geoscience; Aubert et al., 2013, Nature), which reproduce hemispheric asymmetry via reversed-flux patches beneath South Africa driving differential secular variation (Olson & Deguen) and core-mantle boundary heat flux variations (Aubert et al.). (Note: geodynamo simulations use independently measured boundary conditions from seismology, but the hemispheric asymmetry emerges from the physics of fluid convection — it is not fitted to the asymmetry itself.)
patches.rationale: Fix ISS-646: Aubert et al. (2013) citation may not address hemispheric asymmetry specifically. Add Olson & Deguen 2012 as more targeted reference. Reframe data-constrained caveat.
